name: Suriname

outbound_official.note: no official Suriname government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

visa_free.nationality: CARICOM member states (Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Jamaica, Montserrat, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago)
visa_free.notes: Under Article 46 of the Revised Treaty of Chaguaramas; CARICOM passport holders are also exempt from the entry fee. Suriname itself is a CARICOM member. Haiti is a CARICOM member but is refused entry to Suriname (see refused_entry). This entry covers the 13 other CARICOM member states only.
Vatican City passport holders are explicitly listed among categories exempt from both the entry fee and visa requirements per the gov.sr official page and Wikipedia citing Suriname policy.

e_visa.notes: Entry Fee (E-Tourist Card) required; single entry, valid 90 days; must be paid online at suriname.vfsevisa.com at least 24 hours before departure; fee USD 50 or EUR 50 plus VFS service fee USD/EUR 8
Entry Fee (E-Tourist Card) required; single entry, valid 90 days; fee USD 50 or EUR 50

Entry Fee (E-Tourist Card) required; single entry, valid 90 days; fee USD 50 or EUR 50. China's prior visa-free agreement was rescinded 1 July 2022; China is in the entry-fee/e-tourist card category per gov.sr (MFA) official page. Flyslm.com lists China in the full e-visa category but this conflicts with the authoritative gov.sr MFA page which does NOT list China in the 21-country e-visa list. Gov.sr is treated as authoritative.

Flyslm.com lists Colombia as requiring full e-visa, but this conflicts with the authoritative gov.sr MFA source. Gov.sr is treated as authoritative; Colombia classified as entry-fee/e-tourist card.

refused_entry.notes: Entry and transit refused to Haitian citizens, including those who do not disembark and proceed on the same flight. Exception only for Haitian passport holders with legal residence status in Suriname (who must additionally present permission notification from the Suriname government, travel insurance, and proof of financial means). Haiti is technically a CARICOM member but Suriname applies a blanket refusal to Haitian nationals regardless of CARICOM membership. Confirmed as still in effect as of November 2025 per Wikipedia and search sources citing official policy.

visa_policy.notes: As of 1 July 2022, Suriname introduced a unilateral visa abolishment for tourism/family visits up to 90 days, replaced by a mandatory Entry Fee (officially called E-Tourist Card on VFS portal) of USD 50 or EUR 50 plus USD 8 VFS service fee, paid online only. CARICOM passport holders (excluding Haiti - refused entry) are exempt from both visa and entry fee. Vatican City is also explicitly exempt. On 1 May 2023, full visa requirement was reimposed for exactly 21 nationalities (now requiring full e-visa via suriname.vfsevisa.com). Haiti is a CARICOM member but is refused entry to Suriname entirely. All travelers must complete the Immigration and Customs Form (ICF) at icf.sr at least 3 days before arrival (mandatory from 1 November 2024). The entry fee ('e-tourist card') applies to the majority of world passports not covered by the CARICOM exemption or the 21-country e-visa requirement. IMPORTANT: flyslm.com (Suriname Airways) lists 25 countries for full e-visa, including Colombia, Morocco, Romania, China, and Haiti - however the authoritative gov.sr MFA page lists only 21 countries and does NOT include those 5. The gov.sr MFA listing is treated as authoritative; Colombia, Morocco, Romania, and China remain classified as entry-fee/e-tourist card. Haiti remains refused entry (not in e-visa category).

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Diplomatic, Service, Special and Official passports issued by any country are exempt from the Entry Fee requirement and may enter Suriname without paying the entry fee. No specific stay limit stated beyond general 90-day tourist rule.

Bilateral visa exemption agreement between India and Suriname for holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service Passports. Agreement signed 28 April 2017; entered into force 23 February 2019. Maximum stay: 30 days. Indian ordinary passport holders require full e-visa. Source: India Ministry of External Affairs bilateral visa waiver agreement list (mea.gov.in).

cbi.notes: No official CBI program exists. Suriname's President announced in September 2022 that a group of experts was studying the possibility of a CBI program (per news sources), but no formal program has been launched as of 2026. No official government page confirms any active CBI program.

programs.name: One Stop Unit (OSU)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals requiring Short-Stay Visa (MKV), Work Permit, and Residence Permit simultaneously
programs.processing_time: Approximately 3-4 weeks (versus 6+ months sequentially) assuming complete documentation
programs.notes: Launched by Suriname's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, International Business, and International Cooperation; announced October 2024, became operational mid-2025. Additional government fee of USD 1,500 beyond standard fees. All three applications (Short-Stay Visa, Work Permit, Residence Permit) reviewed concurrently. Source is Fragomen immigration alert citing the Suriname MFA; no direct official government URL confirmed.

agreements: CARICOM freedom of movement (Article 46, Revised Treaty of Chaguaramas) - applicable to 13 CARICOM members; Haiti excluded by Suriname's entry refusal
India-Suriname Visa Exemption Agreement for Diplomatic, Official and Service Passport holders (signed 28.04.2017, in force 23.02.2019)

validation_notes: [2026-06-29] RE-VERIFIED: Core access policy confirmed unchanged — 21-country full e-visa list per gov.sr MFA and Wikipedia (confirmed live), CARICOM 13 members + Vatican City visa-free, all other nationalities entry-fee/e-tourist card. Sources loaded: gov.sr MFA page, flyslm.com, Wikipedia, gov.sr Multiple Entry Fee announcement, web search confirming VFS fee structure.

[2026-06-29] FEE UPDATE: VFS Global service fee increased from USD/EUR 8 to USD 18 / EUR 17. Total single-entry cost is now approximately USD 68 / EUR 67. Confirmed via VFS web search results citing official VFS portal.

[2026-06-29] NEW VISA TYPE ADDED: Multiple Entry Fee (E-Tourist Card, 5 entries, 3-month validity) introduced. Pilot launched 1 January 2025 for Brazil and French Guiana nationals; expanded to all eligible entry-fee nationalities from 1 May 2025. Cost: USD 75 / EUR 75 + service fee. Source: gov.sr official announcement at https://gov.sr/introductie-multiple-entry-fee/

[2026-06-29] NOTE ON PHYSICAL PAYMENT: gov.sr MFA page now lists Suriname's Consulate-General in Cayenne and the Saint Laurent office as locations where physical payment is possible (in addition to online). The record's prior note that 'payment on arrival is no longer possible as of January 2023' referred specifically to the airport — consulate/office payment was available for the pilot. This is reflected in the updated E-Tourist Card notes.

[2026-06-29] FLYSLM 25-COUNTRY DISCREPANCY: flyslm.com still lists 25 countries for full e-visa (adds Colombia, Morocco, Romania, China, Haiti to the gov.sr 21). Web search results confirming the official VFS/gov.sr position list exactly 21 countries; Colombia, Morocco, Romania, and China remain entry-fee category. The gov.sr 21-country list is treated as authoritative. Discrepancy persists and is retained in gaps.

[2026-06-27] RE-VERIFIED: Core policy confirmed unchanged - 21-country full e-visa list per gov.sr MFA, CARICOM + Vatican City visa-free, all other nationalities entry-fee/e-tourist card (USD/EUR 50 + USD/EUR 8 VFS fee).

[2026-06-27] DISCREPANCY NOTED: flyslm.com (Suriname Airways official carrier) now lists 25 countries requiring full e-visa, adding Colombia, Morocco, Romania, China (People's Republic), and Haiti to the prior 21. The gov.sr MFA page and Wikipedia still cite exactly 21 countries and do NOT include these 5. Gov.sr MFA is treated as the primary authority; Colombia, Morocco, Romania, and China remain classified as entry-fee/e-tourist card. Haiti remains refused entry. This discrepancy is flagged in gaps for monitoring.

[2026-06-27] CONFIRMED: Haiti refused-entry status still in effect as of November 2025 per Wikipedia and web search results citing official Suriname policy. The flyslm travel-requirements page now describes Haitian residents (with Suriname permits) as having additional documentation requirements - this is consistent with the prior record's exception clause, not a reversal of the ban.
[2026-06-27] UPDATED: Vatican City source_url upgraded from Wikipedia to gov.sr MFA (source_official changed to true) - the gov.sr MFA page lists Vatican City among entry-fee-exempt categories.

[2026-06-27] CONFIRMED: ICF mandatory from 1 November 2024; submission window is 3 days before arrival (updated from 7 days stated in prior notes). Source: flyslm.com travel-requirements page and icf.sr.
CORRECTION (prior run): Haiti reclassified from 'e_visa required' to 'refused_entry'. Haiti is a CARICOM member but Suriname completely refuses entry (and transit) to Haitian nationals.
CORRECTION (prior run): Haiti removed from the CARICOM visa_free group.
CORRECTION (prior run): Colombia (COL), Morocco (MAR), and Romania (ROU) reclassified from 'e_visa (full e-visa required)' to 'e_visa (entry fee / e-tourist card)'.
CORRECTION (prior run): China (CHN) reclassified from 'e_visa (full e-visa required)' to 'e_visa (entry fee / e-tourist card)'.
CORRECTION (prior run): Syria (SYR) confirmed in e-visa category.
ADDITION (prior run): Vatican City (VAT) added to visa_free.
ADDITION (prior run): Brazil (BRA) added to the e-tourist card (entry fee) category.

STRUCTURAL (prior run): The prior record used a single 'e_visa' array to mix two fundamentally different access types - full e-visa and entry fee/e-tourist card. The revised record separates these into 'e_visa' (entry fee / e-tourist card) and 'evisa_required' (full e-visa application required).
gaps: ACTIVE DISCREPANCY: flyslm.com (Suriname Airways) lists 25 countries requiring full e-visa vs gov.sr MFA 21-country list. The 4 extra countries on flyslm are Colombia, Morocco, Romania, China (People's Republic). If the Suriname government updated policy without updating gov.sr, these 4 could now require full e-visa. Recommend re-checking gov.sr and suriname.vfsevisa.com in 30 days.

suriname.vfsevisa.com returns HTTP 403 on all pages - authoritative country list for e-tourist card eligibility could not be directly confirmed from VFS portal. Fee structure (USD 18 / EUR 17 service fee, USD 75 / EUR 75 Multiple Entry Fee) confirmed via web search citing VFS portal content, not directly from portal.
The full exhaustive list of all countries eligible for the entry fee / e-tourist card could not be scraped from suriname.vfsevisa.com (returns 403). Aggregators cite 44-52 countries.
Full list of bilateral visa exemption agreement countries (Japan, Brazil, Israel, South Korea, Philippines) could not be independently verified from official Suriname sources.
No official government page for RBI/investor residence confirmed; third-party sources indicate a 5-year residency path to permanent residency but no official URL verified.
OSU fast-track program details still only confirmed via Fragomen (not a primary government URL).
No confirmed digital nomad visa or startup visa scheme from official sources.
No held-credential (e.g., valid Schengen/US visa granting Suriname entry) rules confirmed from any official Suriname source.

visa_types.name: E-Tourist Card (Entry Fee - Single Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism and family visits to Suriname. Applies to the majority of world passport holders not covered by CARICOM exemption or the 21-country full e-visa requirement.
visa_types.notes: Fee is USD 50 (or EUR 50) plus USD 18 (EUR 17) VFS Global service fee (service fee increased from USD/EUR 8; confirmed via VFS portal as of 2025). Total single-entry cost is approximately USD 68 / EUR 67. Must be purchased online via suriname.vfsevisa.com at least 24 hours before departure. Physical payment is also available at Suriname's Consulate-General in Cayenne (French Guiana) and the office in Saint Laurent du Maroni per gov.sr MFA page. CARICOM passport holders and holders of diplomatic/service/official/special passports are exempt from this fee. Transit passengers are also exempt. A Multiple Entry Fee option (5 entries over 3 months, USD 75 / EUR 75 + service fee) was introduced from January 1, 2025 (initially for Brazil and France only) and expanded to all eligible nationalities from May 1, 2025 — see separate E-Tourist Card (Multiple Entry Fee) visa type.

visa_types.name: E-Tourist Card (Entry Fee - Multiple Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism and family visits to Suriname with up to 5 entries over a 3-month window. Introduced from 1 January 2025 (pilot for Brazilian and French Guiana nationals), expanded to all entry-fee-eligible nationalities from 1 May 2025.
visa_types.notes: Voucher costs USD 75 (or EUR 75) plus USD 18 (EUR 17) VFS Global service fee; total approximately USD 93 / EUR 92. Valid for 3 months from first arrival, permitting up to 5 entries within that window. After 3 months, voucher expires regardless of remaining entries. Children aged 0-2 years are exempt from all entry fees. Children 3 years and older pay the standard entry fee. Pilot program launched 1 January 2025 for Brazilian citizens and French nationals residing in French Guiana; expanded to all eligible entry-fee nationalities from 1 May 2025 per Suriname Government announcement (gov.sr). Source: https://gov.sr/introductie-multiple-entry-fee/ (official gov.sr announcement).

visa_types.name: E-Visa (Tourist/Visit)
visa_types.purpose: Entry authorization for nationals of 21 countries that had their visa-free status reinstated as a full visa requirement from 1 May 2023. Covers tourism and family visits.
visa_types.notes: Required for nationals of: Afghanistan, Angola, Bangladesh, Cameroon, Cuba, Dominican Republic, Egypt, Eritrea, Ghana, India, Kenya, Nepal, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, South Africa, Sri Lanka, Syria, Tunisia, Venezuela, Zambia. Full adjudicated e-visa application required via suriname.vfsevisa.com; not merely a fee payment. Introduced/reimposed 1 May 2023.

visa_types.name: Business E-Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-term entry for business purposes including attending conferences, training sessions, exploratory visits, and other commercial activities.
visa_types.notes: Business visa applications are processed exclusively electronically via the VFS Global online portal (suriname.vfsvisa.com). Required for business visitors who cannot enter under the standard entry fee/e-tourist card category. Multiple-entry options are available for business purposes.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Work Authorization (MKV - Machtiging voor Kort Verblijf)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes foreign nationals to enter Suriname for short-term work or internship purposes of up to 3 months, or as a precursor authorization for longer work stays.
visa_types.notes: Applied for at a Surinamese embassy or consulate in the applicant's home country. Required for visa-obligated foreigners intending stays longer than 3 months. Also required for work and internship stays. The OSU (One Stop Unit) fast-track program launched in 2024/2025 allows concurrent processing of MKV, Work Permit, and Residence Permit for an additional USD 1,500 government fee, reducing processing from 6+ months to approximately 3-4 weeks.

visa_types.name: Work Permit (Werkvergunning)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes a foreign national to be employed by a Surinamese employer or work in Suriname on a longer-term basis.
visa_types.notes: Typically applied for alongside or after obtaining MKV short-stay authorization. Under the OSU fast-track program, the Work Permit is processed concurrently with MKV and Residence Permit. Employer sponsorship required. Exact fee schedule not published on official government website.

visa_types.name: Temporary Residence Permit (Verblijfsvergunning voor bepaalde tijd)
visa_types.purpose: Grants a foreign national the right to reside in Suriname for a fixed period of up to 2 years, renewable before expiration.
visa_types.notes: Maximum duration is 2 years per permit. Application fee is USD 150 or SRD equivalent at Central Bank daily rate, plus SRD 1.50 stamp duty. Application requires formal request letter to the Minister of Justice and Police, passport copy, proof of means of subsistence, and payment receipt. Renewal must be filed at least 3 months before expiration. Filed in person at the Main Department of Alien Affairs, Ministry of Public Works, mr. J. Lachmonstraat no. 166-168, Paramaribo. Persons of Surinamese origin receive preferential treatment.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Permit (Verblijfsvergunning voor onbepaalde tijd)
visa_types.purpose: Grants a foreign national the right to reside indefinitely in Suriname without need for periodic renewal.
visa_types.notes: Application fee is USD 150 or SRD equivalent at Central Bank daily rate, plus SRD 1.50 stamp duty. Requires prior temporary residence history in Suriname. Filed in person at the Main Department of Alien Affairs. Persons of Surinamese origin are exempt from the short-stay MKV requirement and may apply directly for temporary or permanent residence.

visa_types.name: Transit (Visa-Free Airside/Landside)
visa_types.purpose: Allows passengers transiting through Suriname's Johan Adolf Pengel International Airport or land border crossings to proceed without a visa or entry fee.
visa_types.notes: Transit passengers are explicitly exempt from the Entry Fee (E-Tourist Card). Haitian nationals are refused entry including transit - they are refused even if not disembarking and proceeding on the same flight. All other nationalities in transit are exempt from the USD 50 entry fee. No transit visa category formally named; transit exemption is from the general entry fee rule.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Official/Service Passport Entry (Fee-Exempt)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for holders of diplomatic, official, service, or special passports of any nationality, who are exempt from the Entry Fee requirement.
visa_types.notes: Holders of Diplomatic, Service, Special and Official passports from any country are exempt from the Entry Fee. The general 90-day tourism stay limit applies. Indian holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports benefit from a bilateral visa exemption agreement (signed 28 April 2017, in force 23 February 2019) allowing stays of up to 30 days without visa.
